findings.message: uvx invokes 'code-review-graph' without a version pin; each session fetches and runs the latest published PyPI release, so a compromised or broken release is auto-adopted
"significant" is a vague quantifier: 'This saves significant tokens by avoiding full codebase scans'
npm dependencies use caret ranges (better-sqlite3 ^12.4.1, d3 ^7.9.0, esbuild ^0.20.0) without a committed lockfile, allowing unexpected minor/patch upgrades on install

Makes outbound HTTP requests to third-party APIs (MiniMax, Google Gemini, OpenAI-compatible endpoints) carrying API keys from environment variables; code content is included in request bodies when embeddings are computed
Notes section lists 14 supported languages but CLAUDE.md architecture documents 19, explicitly naming Dart, R, Perl, Lua, and Jupyter/Databricks notebooks as additional languages
build-graph Notes lists 14 languages; CLAUDE.md documents 19 — the skill's language list has diverged from the canonical architecture documentation
Skill has no output format section; steps describe what tools to run but do not specify what the final debugging report should contain or how to structure it
"related" is a vague quantifier: 'find code related to the issue' does not constrain the search scope
Skill has no output format section; no guidance on what the exploration summary should contain or how to present findings
"complex" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'identify complex code' gives no measurable threshold
Skill has no output format section; no template for the refactoring plan or safety report
"major" is a vague quantifier in Safety Checks: 'before major refactors' sets no actionable threshold
"many" is a vague quantifier in step 3: 'Files with many dependents (high-risk changes)' — no threshold defined

review-delta and review-pr use the '_tool' suffix for MCP tool names (get_review_context_tool, query_graph_tool, build_or_update_graph_tool) while debug-issue, explore-codebase, and review-changes use bare names; CLAUDE.md Key Tools table also uses bare names
"significant" is a vague quantifier in step 5: 'files with significant changes' does not define what qualifies
"large" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'For large PRs' sets no actionable threshold
"related" is a vague quantifier in Tips: 'find related code the PR might have missed'
Skill has no output format section; steps describe what tools to run but do not specify what the final debugging report should contain or how to structure it
"related" is a vague quantifier: 'find code related to the issue' does not constrain the search scope
Skill has no output format section; no guidance on what the exploration summary should contain or how to present findings
